1→# This Makefile automates the build, test, and clean processes for the project.
     2→# It provides a convenient way to run common tasks using the 'make' command.
     3→# It is designed to work with the 'uv' tool for managing Python environments and dependencies.
     4→# Run `make help` to see all available recipes.
     5→
     6→.SILENT:
     7→.ONESHELL:
     8→.PHONY: \
     9→	setup_uv setup_prod setup_dev setup_claude_code setup_sandbox \
    10→	setup_bert_model setup_plantuml setup_pdf_converter setup_npm_tools setup_lychee \
    11→	setup_ollama clean_ollama setup_dataset \
    12→	dataset_smallest app_quickstart \
    13→	ollama_start ollama_stop \
    14→	plantuml_serve plantuml_render \
    15→	pandoc_run writeup writeup_generate \
    16→	lint_links lint_md \
    17→	app_cli app_gui app_sweep app_batch_run app_profile \
    18→	cc_run_solo cc_collect_teams cc_run_teams \
    19→	lint_src lint_tests complexity duplication \
    20→	test test_rerun test_coverage test_fix_snapshots type_check validate quick_validate \
    21→	setup_phoenix phoenix_start phoenix_stop phoenix_status \
    22→	ralph_userstory ralph_prd_md ralph_prd_json ralph_init ralph_run \
    23→	ralph_worktree ralph_run_worktree ralph_stop ralph_status ralph_watch ralph_get_log ralph_clean \
    24→	clean_results clean_logs \
    25→	help
    26→.DEFAULT_GOAL := help
    27→
    28→
    29→# -- paths --
    30→OUTPUT_BASE := _Agents-eval
    31→SRC_PATH := src
    32→APP_PATH := $(SRC_PATH)/app
    33→CLI_PATH := $(SRC_PATH)/run_cli.py
    34→CONFIG_PATH := $(APP_PATH)/config
    35→GUI_PATH_ST := $(SRC_PATH)/run_gui.py
    36→CHAT_CFG_FILE := $(CONFIG_PATH)/config_chat.json
    37→
    38→# -- ollama (local LLM) --
    39→OLLAMA_SETUP_URL := https://ollama.com/install.sh
    40→OLLAMA_MODEL_NAME := $$(jq -r '.providers.ollama.model_name' $(CHAT_CFG_FILE))
    41→
    42→# -- plantuml (diagram generation) --
    43→PLANTUML_CONTAINER := plantuml/plantuml:latest
    44→PLANTUML_SCRIPT := scripts/writeup/generate-plantuml-png.sh
    45→
    46→# -- pandoc / writeup --
    47→PANDOC_SCRIPT := scripts/writeup/run-pandoc.sh
    48→PDF_CONVERTER_SCRIPT := scripts/writeup/setup-pdf-converter.sh
    49→# pandoc_run optional overrides (empty = disabled)
    50→BIBLIOGRAPHY :=
    51→CSL :=
    52→LIST_OF_FIGURES :=
    53→LIST_OF_TABLES :=
    54→UNNUMBERED_TITLE :=
    55→# writeup recipe overrides
    56→WRITEUP_DIR ?= docs/write-up
    57→WRITEUP_OUTPUT ?= $(WRITEUP_DIR)/writeup.pdf
    58→WRITEUP_BIB ?= $(WRITEUP_DIR)/09a_bibliography.bib
    59→WRITEUP_CSL ?= scripts/writeup/citation-styles/ieee.csl
    60→WRITEUP_PUML_DIR := docs/arch_vis
    61→SKIP_PUML ?=
    62→SKIP_CONTENT ?= 1
    63→WRITEUP_TIMEOUT ?= 600
    64→
    65→# -- phoenix (trace viewer) --
    66→PHOENIX_CONTAINER_NAME := phoenix-tracing
    67→PHOENIX_IMAGE := arizephoenix/phoenix:latest
    68→PHOENIX_PORT := 6006
    69→PHOENIX_GRPC_PORT := 4317
    70→
    71→# -- cc baselines (Claude Code artifact collection) --
    72→CC_TRACES_SCRIPT := scripts/collect-cc-traces
    73→CC_TIMEOUT ?= 300
    74→CC_TEAMS_TIMEOUT ?= 600
    75→CC_MODEL ?=
    76→
    77→# -- ralph (autonomous loop) --
    78→RALPH_PROJECT ?= $(notdir $(CURDIR))
    79→RALPH_TIMEOUT ?=
    80→TEAMS ?= false
    81→
    82→# -- quiet mode (default: quiet; set VERBOSE=1 for full output) --
    83→VERBOSE ?=
    84→ifndef VERBOSE
    85→  RUFF_QUIET   := --quiet
    86→  PYTEST_QUIET := -q --tb=short --no-header
    87→  COV_QUIET    := --cov-report=
    88→  CPLX_QUIET   := -q
    89→endif
    90→
    91→
    92→# MARK: SETUP
    93→
    94→
    95→setup_uv:  ## Install uv and sync frozen deps (minimal bootstrap, used by prebuild)
    96→	pip install uv -q
    97→	uv sync --frozen
    98→
    99→setup_prod:  ## Install uv and deps. Flags: OLLAMA=1
   100→	echo "Setting up prod environment ..."
   101→	$(MAKE) -s setup_uv
   102→	$(if $(filter 1,$(OLLAMA)),$(MAKE) -s setup_ollama && $(MAKE) -s ollama_start)
   103→
   104→setup_dev:  ## Install uv and deps, claude code, mdlint, jscpd, lychee, plantuml. Flags: OLLAMA=1
   105→	echo "Setting up dev environment ..."
   106→	# sudo apt-get install -y gh
   107→	$(MAKE) -s setup_uv
   108→	uv sync
   109→	echo "npm version: $$(npm --version)"
   110→	$(MAKE) -s setup_claude_code
   111→	$(MAKE) -s setup_npm_tools
   112→	$(MAKE) -s setup_lychee
   113→	$(MAKE) -s setup_plantuml
   114→	$(MAKE) -s setup_bert_model
   115→	$(if $(filter 1,$(OLLAMA)),$(MAKE) -s setup_ollama && $(MAKE) -s ollama_start)
   116→
   117→setup_bert_model:  ## Pre-download distilbert-base-uncased for BERTScore (Tier 1 semantic similarity)
   118→	echo "Pre-downloading BERTScore model (distilbert-base-uncased) ..."
   119→	uv run python -c "from bert_score import BERTScorer; BERTScorer(model_type='distilbert-base-uncased', lang='en'); print('BERTScore model ready.')"
   120→
   121→setup_claude_code:  ## Setup claude code CLI
   122→	echo "Setting up Claude Code CLI ..."
   123→	cp -r .claude/.claude.json ~/.claude.json
   124→	curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
   125→	echo "Claude Code CLI version: $$(claude --version)"
   126→
   127→setup_sandbox:  ## Install sandbox deps (bubblewrap, socat) for Linux/WSL2
   128→	# Required for Claude Code sandboxing on Linux/WSL2:
   129→	# - bubblewrap: Provides filesystem and process isolation
   130→	# - socat: Handles network socket communication for sandbox proxy
   131→	# Without these, sandbox falls back to unsandboxed execution (security risk)
   132→	# https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sandboxing
   133→	# https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings
   134→	# https://code.claude.com/docs/en/security
   135→	echo "Installing sandbox dependencies ..."
   136→	if command -v apt-get > /dev/null; then
   137→		sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y bubblewrap socat
   138→	elif command -v dnf > /dev/null; then
   139→		sudo dnf install -y bubblewrap socat
   140→	else
   141→		echo "Unsupported package manager. Install bubblewrap and socat manually."
   142→		exit 1
   143→	fi
   144→	echo "Sandbox dependencies installed."
   145→
   146→setup_plantuml:  ## Setup PlantUML with docker, $(PLANTUML_SCRIPT) and $(PLANTUML_CONTAINER)
   147→	chmod +x $(PLANTUML_SCRIPT)
   148→	if ! command -v plantuml >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   149→		echo "Setting up PlantUML ..."
   150→		sudo apt-get -yyqq update
   151→		sudo apt-get -yyqq install plantuml graphviz
   152→	else
   153→		echo "PlantUML already installed"
   154→	fi
   155→	plantuml -version | grep "PlantUML version"
   156→
   157→setup_pdf_converter:  ## Setup PDF converter tools. Usage: make setup_pdf_converter CONVERTER=pandoc | For help: make setup_pdf_converter HELP
   158→	if [ -n "$(HELP)" ] || [ "$(origin HELP)" = "command line" ]; then
   159→		$(PDF_CONVERTER_SCRIPT) help
   160→	else
   161→		chmod +x $(PDF_CONVERTER_SCRIPT)
   162→		$(PDF_CONVERTER_SCRIPT) "$(CONVERTER)"
   163→	fi
   164→
   165→# TODO: evaluate Python-native alternatives (pymarkdownlnt, mdformat, pylint R0801) to reduce npm dependency
   166→setup_npm_tools:  ## Setup npm-based dev tools (markdownlint, jscpd). Requires node.js and npm
   167→	echo "Setting up npm dev tools ..."
   168→	npm install -gs markdownlint-cli jscpd
   169→	echo "markdownlint version: $$(markdownlint --version)"
   170→	echo "jscpd version: $$(jscpd --version)"
   171→
   172→setup_lychee:  ## Install lychee link checker (Rust binary, requires sudo)
   173→	curl -sL https://github.com/lycheeverse/lychee/releases/latest/download/lychee-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz | sudo tar xz -C /usr/local/bin lychee
   174→	echo "lychee version: $$(lychee --version)"
   175→
   176→# Ollama BINDIR in /usr/local/bin /usr/bin /bin
   177→setup_ollama:  ## Download Ollama, script does start local Ollama server
   178→	echo "Downloading Ollama binary ... Using '$(OLLAMA_SETUP_URL)'."
   179→	# script does start server but not consistently
   180→	curl -fsSL $(OLLAMA_SETUP_URL) | sh
   181→	echo "Pulling model '$(OLLAMA_MODEL_NAME)' ..."
   182→	ollama pull $(OLLAMA_MODEL_NAME)
   183→
   184→clean_ollama:  ## Remove local Ollama from system
   185→	echo "Searching for Ollama binary ..."
   186→	BIN=""
   187→	for BINDIR in /usr/local/bin /usr/bin /bin; do
   188→		if [ -x "$$BINDIR/ollama" ]; then
   189→			echo "Ollama binary found in '$$BINDIR'"
   190→			BIN="$$BINDIR/ollama"
   191→			break
   192→		fi
   193→	done
   194→	if [ -z "$$BIN" ]; then
   195→		echo "Ollama binary not found in PATH"
   196→		exit 1
   197→	fi
   198→	echo "Removing $$BIN ..."
   199→	sudo rm -f "$$BIN"
   200→
   201→setup_dataset:  ## Download PeerRead dataset. Usage: make setup_dataset [MODE=full] [MAX_PAPERS=5]
   202→	$(if $(filter full,$(MODE)),\
   203→		echo "Downloading full PeerRead dataset ..." && \
   204→		$(MAKE) -s app_cli ARGS=--download-peerread-full-only,\
   205→		echo "Downloading PeerRead sample ..." && \
   206→		$(MAKE) -s app_cli ARGS="--download-peerread-samples-only $(if $(MAX_PAPERS),--peerread-max-papers-per-sample-download $(MAX_PAPERS))")
   207→	$(MAKE) -s dataset_smallest
   208→
   209→dataset_smallest:  ## Show N smallest papers by file size. Usage: make dataset_smallest N=5
   210→	@find $(OUTPUT_BASE)/datasets/peerread -path "*/parsed_pdfs/*.json" \
   211→		-type f -printf '%s %p\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -n | head -$(or $(N),10)
   212→
   213→setup_dataset_sample:  ## Download small sample of PeerRead dataset
   214→	echo "Downloading small sample of PeerRead dataset ..."
   215→	$(MAKE) -s app_cli ARGS=--download-peerread-samples-only
   216→	$(MAKE) -s dataset_smallest
   217→
   218→# MARK: OLLAMA
   219→
   220→
   221→ollama_start:  ## Start local Ollama server, default 127.0.0.1:11434
   222→	ollama serve
   223→
   224→ollama_stop:  ## Stop local Ollama server
   225→	echo "Stopping Ollama server ..."
   226→	pkill ollama
   227→
   228→
   229→# MARK: PLANTUML
   230→
   231→
   232→plantuml_serve:  ## Start PlantUML server for interactive diagram editing
   233→	# https://github.com/plantuml/plantuml-server
   234→	# plantuml/plantuml-server:tomcat
   235→	docker run -d -p 8080:8080 "$(PLANTUML_CONTAINER)"
   236→
   237→plantuml_render:  ## Render a themed diagram from a PlantUML file
   238→	$(PLANTUML_SCRIPT) "$(INPUT_FILE)" "$(STYLE)" "$(OUTPUT_PATH)" \
   239→		"$(CHECK_ONLY)" "$(PLANTUML_CONTAINER)"
   240→
   241→
   242→# MARK: PANDOC
   243→
   244→
   245→pandoc_run:  ## Convert MD to PDF using pandoc. Usage: dir=docs/en && make pandoc_run INPUT_FILES="$$(printf '%s\\036' $$dir/*.md)" OUTPUT_FILE="$$dir/report.pdf" [BIBLIOGRAPHY="$$dir/refs.bib"] [CSL="$$dir/style.csl"] | Help: make pandoc_run HELP=1
   246→	if [ -n "$(HELP)" ]; then
   247→		$(PANDOC_SCRIPT) help
   248→	else
   249→		chmod +x $(PANDOC_SCRIPT)
   250→		$(PANDOC_SCRIPT) "$(INPUT_FILES)" "$(OUTPUT_FILE)" \
   251→			"$(TITLE_PAGE)" "$(TEMPLATE)" "$(FOOTER_TEXT)" \
   252→			"$(TOC_TITLE)" "$(LANGUAGE)" "$(NUMBER_SECTIONS)" \
   253→			"$(BIBLIOGRAPHY)" "$(CSL)" \
   254→			"$(LIST_OF_FIGURES)" "$(LIST_OF_TABLES)" "$(UNNUMBERED_TITLE)"
   255→	fi
   256→
   257→
   258→# Convenience wrapper: content generation (CC teams) + PlantUML regen + pandoc PDF build.
   259→writeup:  ## Build writeup PDF. Usage: make writeup WRITEUP_DIR=docs/write-up/bs-new [LANGUAGE=de-DE] [SKIP_CONTENT=1] [SKIP_PUML=1]
   260→	if [ -z "$(SKIP_CONTENT)" ]; then
   261→		echo "=== Generating writeup content with Claude Code teams ==="
   262→		$(MAKE) -s writeup_generate
   263→	fi
   264→	if [ -z "$(SKIP_PUML)" ]; then
   265→		echo "=== Regenerating PlantUML diagrams ==="
   266→		for f in $(WRITEUP_PUML_DIR)/*.plantuml $(WRITEUP_PUML_DIR)/*.puml; do
   267→			[ -f "$$f" ] || continue
   268→			echo "  Processing $$f ..."
   269→			$(MAKE) -s plantuml_render INPUT_FILE="$$f" STYLE="light" OUTPUT_PATH="assets/images"
   270→		done
   271→	fi
   272→	echo "=== Building writeup PDF ==="
   273→	$(MAKE) -s pandoc_run \
   274→		INPUT_FILES="$$(printf '%s\036' $(WRITEUP_DIR)/01_*.md $(WRITEUP_DIR)/0[2-8]_*.md $(WRITEUP_DIR)/09b_*.md $(WRITEUP_DIR)/10_*.md $(WRITEUP_DIR)/11_*.md)" \
   275→		OUTPUT_FILE="$(WRITEUP_OUTPUT)" \
   276→		BIBLIOGRAPHY="$(WRITEUP_BIB)" \
   277→		CSL="$(WRITEUP_CSL)" \
   278→		LANGUAGE="$(LANGUAGE)" \
   279→		NUMBER_SECTIONS="true" \
   280→		LIST_OF_FIGURES="true" \
   281→		LIST_OF_TABLES="false" \
   282→		UNNUMBERED_TITLE="true"
   283→	echo "=== Writeup PDF: $(WRITEUP_OUTPUT) ==="
   284→
   285→# Generate writeup content using CC teams + /generating-writeup skill.
   286→writeup_generate:  ## Generate writeup markdown via CC teams. Usage: make writeup_generate WRITEUP_DIR=docs/write-up/bs-new [WRITEUP_TIMEOUT=600] [CC_MODEL=sonnet]
   287→	echo "=== Generating writeup content (timeout: $(WRITEUP_TIMEOUT)s) ==="
   288→	mkdir -p "$(WRITEUP_DIR)"
   289→	CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 \
   290→	timeout $(WRITEUP_TIMEOUT) claude -p \
   291→		"/generating-writeup $(notdir $(WRITEUP_DIR)) IEEE -- Use agent teams for parallel chapter creation. Target: $(WRITEUP_DIR)" \
   292→		--output-format stream-json --verbose \
   293→		$(if $(CC_MODEL),--model $(CC_MODEL)) \
   294→		> "$(WRITEUP_DIR)/generate.jsonl" 2>&1 \
   295→		|| { EXIT_CODE=$$?; [ $$EXIT_CODE -eq 124 ] && echo "Content generation timed out after $(WRITEUP_TIMEOUT)s"; exit $$EXIT_CODE; }
   296→	echo "=== Content generation complete. Output: $(WRITEUP_DIR)/generate.jsonl ==="
   297→
   298→
   299→# MARK: MARKDOWN
   300→
   301→
   302→lint_links:  ## Check for broken links with lychee. Usage: make lint_links [INPUT_FILES="docs/**/*.md"]
   303→	if command -v lychee > /dev/null 2>&1; then
   304→		lychee $(or $(INPUT_FILES),.)
   305→	else
   306→		echo "lychee not installed — skipping link check (run 'make setup_lychee' to install)"
   307→	fi
   308→
   309→lint_md:  ## Lint markdown files. Usage: make lint_md INPUT_FILES="docs/**/*.md"
   310→	if [ -z "$(INPUT_FILES)" ]; then
   311→		echo "Error: No input files specified. Use INPUT_FILES=\"docs/**/*.md\""
   312→		exit 1
   313→	fi
   314→	markdownlint $(INPUT_FILES) --fix
   315→
   316→
   317→# MARK: APP
   318→
   319→
   320→app_quickstart:  ## Download sample data and run evaluation on smallest paper
   321→	echo "=== Quick Start: Download samples + evaluate smallest paper ==="
   322→	if [ ! -d $(OUTPUT_BASE)/datasets/peerread ]; then
   323→		$(MAKE) -s setup_dataset
   324→	else
   325→		echo "PeerRead dataset already present, skipping download."
   326→	fi
   327→	PAPER_ID=$$($(MAKE) -s dataset_smallest N=1 \
   328→		| awk '{print $$2}' | sed 's|.*/parsed_pdfs/||;s|\.pdf\.json||')
   329→	if [ -z "$$PAPER_ID" ]; then
   330→		echo "ERROR: No papers found. Run 'make setup_dataset' first."
   331→		exit 1
   332→	fi
   333→	echo "Selected smallest paper: $$PAPER_ID"
   334→	$(MAKE) -s app_cli ARGS="--paper-id=$$PAPER_ID"
   335→
   336→
   337→app_cli:  ## Run app on CLI only. Usage: make app_cli ARGS="--help" or make app_cli ARGS="--download-peerread-samples-only"
   338→	PYTHONPATH=$(SRC_PATH) uv run python $(CLI_PATH) $(ARGS)
   339→
   340→app_gui:  ## Run app with Streamlit GUI
   341→	PYTHONPATH=$(SRC_PATH) uv run streamlit run $(GUI_PATH_ST)
   342→
   343→app_sweep:  ## Run MAS composition sweep. Usage: make app_sweep ARGS="--paper-ids 1,2,3 --repetitions 3 --all-compositions"
   344→	PYTHONPATH=$(SRC_PATH) uv run python $(SRC_PATH)/run_sweep.py $(ARGS)
   345→
   346→app_batch_eval:  ## Re-evaluate existing runs and regenerate sweep summaries. Usage: make app_batch_eval ARGS="--runs-only"
   347→	uv run python scripts/batch_eval.py $(ARGS)
   348→
   349→app_batch_run:  ## Run app_cli for all agent compositions. Usage: make app_batch_run ARGS="--paper-ids 1105.1072 [--parallel 4]"
   350→	uv run python scripts/batch_run.py $(ARGS)
   351→
   352→app_profile:  ## Profile app with scalene
   353→	mkdir -p $(OUTPUT_BASE)/logs/scalene-profiles
   354→	uv run scalene --outfile \
   355→		"$(OUTPUT_BASE)/logs/scalene-profiles/profile-$$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)" \
   356→		"$(CLI_PATH)"
   357→
   358→app_clean_results:  ## Remove all sweep result files
   359→	echo "Removing $(OUTPUT_BASE)/output/sweeps/ contents ..."
   360→	rm -rf $(OUTPUT_BASE)/output/sweeps/*
   361→	echo "Sweep results cleaned."
   362→
   363→app_clean_logs:  ## Remove accumulated agent evaluation logs
   364→	echo "WARNING: This will delete all logs in $(OUTPUT_BASE)/logs/ (including traces)!"
   365→	echo "Press Ctrl+C to cancel, Enter to continue..."
   366→	read
   367→	rm -rf $(OUTPUT_BASE)/logs/*
   368→	echo "Agent evaluation logs cleaned."
   369→
   370→
   371→# MARK: CC-BASELINES
   372→
   373→
   374→cc_run_solo:  ## Run CC solo via Python entry point. Usage: make cc_run_solo PAPER_ID=1105.1072 [CC_TIMEOUT=300]
   375→	if [ -z "$(PAPER_ID)" ]; then
   376→		echo "Error: PAPER_ID required. Usage: make cc_run_solo PAPER_ID=1105.1072"
   377→		exit 1
   378→	fi
   379→	uv run python $(CLI_PATH) \
   380→		--engine cc \
   381→		--paper-id "$(PAPER_ID)"
   382→
   383→cc_collect_teams:  ## Collect existing CC teams artifacts (stub — use cc_run_teams instead)
   384→	echo "Note: Use 'make cc_run_teams' to run CC in teams mode via the Python engine."
   385→	echo "Direct artifact collection is no longer supported (shell scripts removed)."
   386→
   387→cc_run_teams:  ## Run CC teams via Python entry point. Usage: make cc_run_teams PAPER_ID=1105.1072 [CC_TEAMS_TIMEOUT=600]
   388→	if [ -z "$(PAPER_ID)" ]; then
   389→		echo "Error: PAPER_ID required. Usage: make cc_run_teams PAPER_ID=1105.1072"
   390→		exit 1
   391→	fi
   392→	uv run python $(CLI_PATH) \
   393→		--engine cc \
   394→		--cc-teams \
   395→		--paper-id "$(PAPER_ID)"
   396→
   397→
   398→# MARK: QUALITY
   399→
   400→
   401→lint_src:  ## Lint and format src with ruff
   402→	echo "--- lint_src$(if $(RUFF_QUIET), [quiet])"
   403→	uv run ruff format $(RUFF_QUIET) --exclude tests
   404→	uv run ruff check $(RUFF_QUIET) --fix --exclude tests
   405→
   406→lint_tests:  ## Lint and format tests with ruff
   407→	echo "--- lint_tests$(if $(RUFF_QUIET), [quiet])"
   408→	uv run ruff format $(RUFF_QUIET) tests
   409→	uv run ruff check $(RUFF_QUIET) tests --fix
   410→
   411→complexity:  ## Check cognitive complexity with complexipy
   412→	echo "--- complexity$(if $(CPLX_QUIET), [quiet])"

    94→def parse_args(argv: list[str]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    95→    """Parse command line arguments into a dictionary.

   110→def _run_cc_engine(args: dict[str, Any], cc_teams: bool) -> Any:
   111→    """Run the Claude Code engine and return the result object.
   112→
   113→    Args:
   114→        args: Parsed CLI arguments dict (mutated: cc_solo_dir may be set).
   115→        cc_teams: Whether to use Agent Teams mode.
   116→
   117→    Returns:
   118→        CCResult object from the engine run.
   119→    """
   120→    from app.engines.cc_engine import build_cc_query, run_cc_solo, run_cc_teams
   121→
   122→    query = build_cc_query(args.get("query", ""), args.get("paper_id"), cc_teams=cc_teams)
   123→    cc_result_obj = (
   124→        run_cc_teams(query, timeout=600) if cc_teams else run_cc_solo(query, timeout=600)
   125→    )
   126→
   127→    if cc_result_obj.session_dir:
   128→        args["cc_solo_dir"] = cc_result_obj.session_dir
   129→
   130→    return cc_result_obj

   182→
   183→    if engine == "cc" and not shutil.which("claude"):
   184→        print(

   186→            file=sys.stderr,

   199→

    90→async def _run_agent_execution(
    91→    chat_config_file: str | Path,
    92→    chat_provider: str,
    93→    query: str,
    94→    paper_id: str | None,
    95→    enable_review_tools: bool,
    96→    include_researcher: bool,
    97→    include_analyst: bool,
    98→    include_synthesiser: bool,
    99→    token_limit: int | None,
   100→    execution_id: str | None = None,
   101→) -> tuple[str, dict[str, str], Any, str]:
   102→    """Execute agent system and return execution ID, prompts, manager output, and chat model.
   103→
   104→    Args:
   105→        chat_config_file: Path to chat configuration file.
   106→        chat_provider: LLM provider name.
   107→        query: User query string.
   108→        paper_id: Optional PeerRead paper ID.
   109→        enable_review_tools: Whether to enable review tools.
   110→        include_researcher: Whether to include researcher agent.
   111→        include_analyst: Whether to include analyst agent.
   112→        include_synthesiser: Whether to include synthesiser agent.
   113→        token_limit: Optional token limit override.
   114→        execution_id: Optional pre-generated execution ID forwarded to run_manager.
   115→
   116→    Returns:
   117→        Tuple of (execution_id, prompts dict, manager_output, chat_model).
   118→    """
   119→    chat_config = load_config(chat_config_file, ChatConfig)
   120→    prompts: dict[str, str] = cast(dict[str, str], chat_config.prompts)  # type: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]
   121→
   122→    query, review_tools_enabled = _prepare_query(paper_id, query, prompts)
   123→    enable_review_tools = enable_review_tools or review_tools_enabled
   124→
   125→    chat_env_config = AppEnv()
   126→    agent_env = setup_agent_env(chat_provider, query, chat_config, chat_env_config, token_limit)
   127→
   128→    login(PROJECT_NAME, chat_env_config)
   129→    _initialize_instrumentation()
   130→
   131→    manager = get_manager(
   132→        agent_env.provider,
   133→        agent_env.provider_config,
   134→        agent_env.api_key,
   135→        agent_env.prompts,
   136→        include_researcher,
   137→        include_analyst,
   138→        include_synthesiser,
   139→        enable_review_tools=enable_review_tools,
   140→    )
   141→    execution_id, manager_output = await run_manager(
   142→        manager,
   143→        agent_env.query,
   144→        agent_env.provider,
   145→        agent_env.usage_limits,
   146→        execution_id=execution_id,
   147→    )
   148→
   149→    return execution_id, prompts, manager_output, agent_env.provider_config.model_name

   186→def _prepare_query(paper_id: str | None, query: str, prompts: dict[str, str]) -> tuple[str, bool]:

   188→    if paper_id:

   199→        query = input(f"{default_prompt} ")

   201→    return query, False

   385→async def main(
   386→    chat_provider: str = CHAT_DEFAULT_PROVIDER,
   387→    query: str = "",
   388→    include_researcher: bool = False,
   389→    include_analyst: bool = False,
   390→    include_synthesiser: bool = False,
   391→    chat_config_file: str | Path | None = None,
   392→    enable_review_tools: bool = False,
   393→    paper_id: str | None = None,
   394→    skip_eval: bool = False,
   395→    download_peerread_full_only: bool = False,
   396→    download_peerread_samples_only: bool = False,
   397→    peerread_max_papers_per_sample_download: int | None = 5,
   398→    cc_solo_dir: str | None = None,
   399→    cc_teams_dir: str | None = None,
   400→    cc_teams_tasks_dir: str | None = None,
   401→    token_limit: int | None = None,
   402→    judge_settings: JudgeSettings | None = None,
   403→    engine: str = "mas",
   404→    cc_result: Any | None = None,
   405→    cc_teams: bool = False,
   406→    cc_model: str | None = None,
   407→) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   408→    """Main entry point for the application.
   409→
   410→    Returns:
   411→        Dictionary with 'composite_result' (CompositeResult) and 'graph' (nx.DiGraph)
   412→        if evaluation runs successfully, None otherwise (CLI mode or download-only).
   413→    """
   414→    logger.info(f"Starting app '{PROJECT_NAME}' v{__version__} (engine={engine})")
   415→
   416→    if _handle_download_mode(
   417→        download_peerread_full_only,
   418→        download_peerread_samples_only,
   419→        peerread_max_papers_per_sample_download,
   420→    ):
   421→        return None
   422→
   423→    try:
   424→        if chat_config_file is None:
   425→            chat_config_file = resolve_config_path(CHAT_CONFIG_FILE)
   426→        logger.info(f"Chat config file: {chat_config_file}")
   427→
   428→        with span("main()"):
   429→            # Generate execution_id up-front so RunContext is active before engine runs
   430→            execution_id = f"exec_{_uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
   431→            run_ctx = RunContext.create(
   432→                engine_type=_resolve_engine_type(engine, cc_teams),
   433→                paper_id=paper_id or "unknown",
   434→                execution_id=execution_id,
   435→            )
   436→            set_active_run_context(run_ctx)
   437→
   438→            # S10-F1: CC engine branch — skip MAS, use CC result directly
   439→            if engine == "cc" and cc_result is not None:
   440→                composite_result, graph, execution_id = await _run_cc_engine_path(
   441→                    cc_result,
   442→                    skip_eval,
   443→                    paper_id,
   444→                    cc_solo_dir,
   445→                    cc_teams_dir,
   446→                    cc_teams_tasks_dir,
   447→                    chat_provider,
   448→                    judge_settings,
   449→                    cc_teams=cc_teams,
   450→                    run_dir=run_ctx.run_dir,
   451→                    cc_model=cc_model,
   452→                )
   453→            else:
   454→                composite_result, graph, execution_id = await _run_mas_engine_path(
   455→                    chat_config_file,
   456→                    chat_provider,
   457→                    query,
   458→                    paper_id,
   459→                    enable_review_tools,
   460→                    include_researcher,
   461→                    include_analyst,
   462→                    include_synthesiser,
   463→                    token_limit,
   464→                    skip_eval,
   465→                    cc_solo_dir,
   466→                    cc_teams_dir,
   467→                    cc_teams_tasks_dir,
   468→                    judge_settings,
   469→                    execution_id=execution_id,
   470→                    run_dir=run_ctx.run_dir,
   471→                )
   472→
   473→            persist_graph(graph, run_ctx.run_dir)
   474→
   475→            logger.info(f"Exiting app '{PROJECT_NAME}'")
   476→            return _prepare_result_dict(
   477→                composite_result, graph, execution_id, run_context=get_active_run_context()
   478→            )
   479→
   480→    except Exception as e:
   481→        msg = generic_exception(f"Aborting app '{PROJECT_NAME}' with: {e}")
   482→        logger.exception(msg)
   483→        raise Exception(msg) from e
   484→    finally:
   485→        set_active_run_context(None)

    93→def resilient_tool_wrapper(tool: Tool[Any]) -> Tool[Any]:
    94→    """Wrap a PydanticAI Tool so HTTP and network errors return error strings.
    95→
    96→    Search tools are supplementary — when they fail, the agent should receive a
    97→    descriptive error message and continue generating output from paper content
    98→    and model knowledge. This prevents a search outage from crashing the run.
    99→
   100→    Catches:
   101→        - httpx.HTTPStatusError (403 Forbidden, 429 Too Many Requests, etc.)
   102→        - httpx.HTTPError (broader httpx network errors)
   103→        - Exception (any other network or library failure)
   104→
   105→    Args:
   106→        tool: The original PydanticAI Tool to wrap.
   107→
   108→    Returns:
   109→        A new Tool with the same name and description, but with a resilient
   110→        function that catches search errors and returns a descriptive string.
   111→    """
   112→    original_fn: Callable[..., Any] = tool.function
   113→
   114→    async def _resilient(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
   115→        try:
   116→            return await original_fn(*args, **kwargs)
   117→        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as exc:
   118→            status = exc.response.status_code
   119→            url = str(exc.request.url) if exc.request else "unknown"
   120→            logger.warning(f"Search tool '{tool.name}' HTTP {status} error for URL {url}: {exc}")
   121→            return (
   122→                f"Search tool '{tool.name}' is currently unavailable "
   123→                f"(HTTP {status}). Proceed using paper content and model knowledge."
   124→            )
   125→        except httpx.HTTPError as exc:
   126→            logger.warning(f"Search tool '{tool.name}' network error: {exc}")
   127→            return (
   128→                f"Search tool '{tool.name}' is currently unavailable "
   129→                f"(network error). Proceed using paper content and model knowledge."
   130→            )
   131→        except Exception as exc:
   132→            logger.warning(f"Search tool '{tool.name}' failed: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
   133→            return (
   134→                f"Search tool '{tool.name}' is currently unavailable "
   135→                f"({type(exc).__name__}). Proceed using paper content and model knowledge."
   136→            )
   137→
   138→    return Tool(
   139→        _resilient,
   140→        name=tool.name,
   141→        description=tool.description,
   142→    )
   143→

   145→def _validate_model_return(
   146→    result_output: Any,
   147→    result_model: type[ResultBaseType],
   148→) -> ResultBaseType:
   149→    """Validates the output against the expected model.
   150→
   151→    When result_output is a str (e.g. from OpenAI-compatible providers that
   152→    return plain text instead of structured output), tries model_validate_json()
   153→    first. This correctly handles valid JSON strings that model_validate() would
   154→    reject as "not a dict". Invalid JSON strings raise with the original content
   155→    included in the error message for easier debugging.
   156→
   157→    When result_output is a dict or already the correct Pydantic type,
   158→    model_validate() is used as before.
   159→
   160→    Args:
   161→        result_output: The output to validate. May be a JSON string, dict, or
   162→            existing Pydantic model instance.
   163→        result_model: The Pydantic model class to validate against.
   164→
   165→    Returns:
   166→        A validated instance of result_model.
   167→
   168→    Raises:
   169→        ValidationError: If the input cannot be parsed into result_model.
   170→        Exception: For unexpected errors during validation.
   171→    """
   172→    try:
   173→        if isinstance(result_output, str):
   174→            # Reason: model_validate() rejects str inputs even when valid JSON;
   175→            # model_validate_json() handles the JSON string path correctly.
   176→            try:
   177→                return result_model.model_validate_json(result_output)
   178→            except ValidationError as e:
   179→                msg = invalid_data_model_format(
   180→                    f"JSON parsing failed for input '{result_output}': {e}"
   181→                )
   182→                logger.error(msg)
   183→                raise ValueError(msg) from e
   184→        return result_model.model_validate(result_output)
   185→    except (ValidationError, ValueError):
   186→        raise
   187→    except Exception as e:
   188→        msg = generic_exception(str(e))
   189→        logger.exception(msg)
   190→        raise Exception(msg)

   193→async def _execute_traced_delegation(
   194→    sub_agent: Agent[None, BaseModel],
   195→    ctx: RunContext[None],
   196→    query: str,
   197→    *,
   198→    to_agent: str,
   199→    tool_name: str,
   200→    task_type: str,
   201→) -> Any:
   202→    """Execute a sub-agent delegation with trace collection.
   203→
   204→    Centralizes the tracing pattern shared by all delegation tools:
   205→    log coordination event, log interaction, run sub-agent, log tool call with timing.
   206→
   207→    Args:
   208→        sub_agent: The sub-agent to delegate to.
   209→        ctx: The run context from the manager agent.
   210→        query: The query string to delegate.
   211→        to_agent: Target agent name for trace logging.
   212→        tool_name: Tool name for trace logging.
   213→        task_type: Task type for trace logging.
   214→
   215→    Returns:
   216→        The AgentRunResult from the sub-agent execution.
   217→    """
   218→    trace_collector = get_trace_collector()
   219→    start_time = time.perf_counter()
   220→
   221→    trace_collector.log_agent_interaction(
   222→        from_agent="manager",
   223→        to_agent=to_agent,
   224→        interaction_type="delegation",
   225→        data={"query": query, "task_type": task_type},
   226→    )
   227→
   228→    trace_collector.log_coordination_event(
   229→        manager_agent="manager",
   230→        event_type="delegation",
   231→        target_agents=[to_agent],
   232→        data={"query": query, "task_type": task_type},
   233→    )
   234→
   235→    result = await sub_agent.run(query, usage=ctx.usage)
   236→
   237→    duration = time.perf_counter() - start_time
   238→    trace_collector.log_tool_call(
   239→        agent_id="manager",
   240→        tool_name=tool_name,
   241→        success=True,
   242→        duration=duration,
   243→        context=f"{task_type}_delegation",
   244→    )
   245→
   246→    return result
   247→

   392→def _create_optional_agent(
   393→    model: Any,
   394→    output_type: type[BaseModel],
   395→    system_prompt: str,
   396→    tools: list[Tool[Any]] | None = None,
   397→) -> Agent[None, BaseModel] | None:
   398→    """Create an agent if model is provided, otherwise return None.
   399→
   400→    Args:
   401→        model: The model instance, or None to skip creation.
   402→        output_type: Pydantic model type for agent output.
   403→        system_prompt: System prompt string for the agent.
   404→        tools: Optional list of tools to register on the agent.
   405→
   406→    Returns:
   407→        Configured Agent instance, or None if model is None.
   408→    """
   409→    if model is None:
   410→        return None
   411→    config: dict[str, Any] = {
   412→        "model": model,
   413→        "output_type": output_type,
   414→        "system_prompt": system_prompt,
   415→    }
   416→    if tools:
   417→        config["tools"] = tools
   418→    return _create_agent(AgentConfig.model_validate(config))

   421→def _get_result_type(
   422→    provider: str,
   423→    enable_review_tools: bool = False,
   424→) -> type[ResearchResult | ResearchResultSimple | ReviewGenerationResult]:
   425→    """
   426→    Select appropriate result model based on provider and tool configuration.
   427→
   428→    Args:
   429→        provider: The provider name (e.g., 'gemini', 'openai', etc.)
   430→        enable_review_tools: Whether review tools are enabled for paper reviews
   431→
   432→    Returns:
   433→        ReviewGenerationResult when review tools are enabled
   434→        ResearchResultSimple for Gemini (no additionalProperties support)
   435→        ResearchResult for other providers (supports flexible union types)
   436→    """
   437→    # When review tools are enabled, always use ReviewGenerationResult
   438→    if enable_review_tools:
   439→        return ReviewGenerationResult
   440→
   441→    # For research tasks, select based on provider capabilities
   442→    # Gemini doesn't support additionalProperties in JSON schema
   443→    if provider.lower() == "gemini":
   444→        return ResearchResultSimple
   445→    return ResearchResult

   448→def _create_manager(
   449→    prompts: dict[str, str],
   450→    models: ModelDict,
   451→    provider: str,
   452→    enable_review_tools: bool = False,
   453→    max_content_length: int = 15000,
   454→) -> Agent[None, BaseModel]:
   455→    """
   456→    Creates and configures a manager Agent with associated researcher, analyst,
   457→    and optionally synthesiser agents.
   458→    Args:
   459→        prompts (Dict[str, str]): Dictionary containing system prompts for each agent.
   460→        model_manager (GeminiModel | OpenAIModel): Model to be used by the manager
   461→            agent.
   462→        model_researcher (GeminiModel | OpenAIModel | None, optional): Model to be used
   463→            by the researcher agent.
   464→        model_analyst (GeminiModel | OpenAIModel | None, optional): Model to be used by
   465→            the analyst agent. Defaults to None.
   466→        model_synthesiser (GeminiModel | OpenAIModel | None, optional): Model to be used
   467→            by the synthesiser agent. Defaults to None.
   468→    Returns:
   469→        Agent: Configured manager agent with associated tools and agents.
   470→    """
   471→
   472→    status = f"Creating manager({models.model_manager.model_name})"
   473→    active_agents = [
   474→        agent
   475→        for agent in [
   476→            f"researcher({models.model_researcher.model_name})"
   477→            if models.model_researcher
   478→            else None,
   479→            f"analyst({models.model_analyst.model_name})" if models.model_analyst else None,
   480→            f"synthesiser({models.model_synthesiser.model_name})"
   481→            if models.model_synthesiser
   482→            else None,
   483→        ]
   484→        if agent
   485→    ]
   486→    status += f" with agents: {', '.join(active_agents)}" if active_agents else ""
   487→    logger.info(status)
   488→
   489→    # Select appropriate result type based on provider and tool configuration
   490→    result_type = _get_result_type(provider, enable_review_tools)
   491→
   492→    manager = _create_agent(
   493→        AgentConfig.model_validate(
   494→            {
   495→                "model": models.model_manager,
   496→                "output_type": result_type,
   497→                "system_prompt": prompts["system_prompt_manager"],
   498→            }
   499→        )
   500→    )
   501→
   502→    # Reason: prompt lookup guarded by model presence to match original behavior —
   503→    # tests may omit sub-agent prompt keys when model is None.
   504→    researcher = _create_optional_agent(
   505→        models.model_researcher,
   506→        result_type,
   507→        prompts["system_prompt_researcher"] if models.model_researcher else "",
   508→        tools=[resilient_tool_wrapper(duckduckgo_search_tool())],
   509→    )
   510→    analyst = _create_optional_agent(
   511→        models.model_analyst,
   512→        AnalysisResult,
   513→        prompts["system_prompt_analyst"] if models.model_analyst else "",
   514→    )
   515→    synthesiser = _create_optional_agent(
   516→        models.model_synthesiser,
   517→        AnalysisResult,
   518→        prompts["system_prompt_synthesiser"] if models.model_synthesiser else "",
   519→    )
   520→
   521→    _add_tools_to_manager_agent(manager, researcher, analyst, synthesiser, result_type)
   522→
   523→    # Determine target agent for PeerRead tools
   524→    # Researcher gets tools in multi-agent mode, manager in single-agent mode
   525→    target_agent = researcher if researcher is not None else manager
   526→    target_agent_id = "researcher" if researcher is not None else "manager"
   527→
   528→    # Add PeerRead base tools
   529→    add_peerread_tools_to_agent(target_agent, agent_id=target_agent_id)
   530→
   531→    # Add review tools if enabled
   532→    if enable_review_tools:
   533→        from app.tools.peerread_tools import add_peerread_review_tools_to_agent
   534→
   535→        add_peerread_review_tools_to_agent(
   536→            target_agent, agent_id=target_agent_id, max_content_length=max_content_length
   537→        )
   538→
   539→    return manager

   553→    Initializes and returns a Agent manager with the specified configuration.
   554→    Args:

   557→        api_key (str): API key for authentication with the provider.

   560→            Defaults to False.

   562→            Defaults to False.

   574→            "provider_config": provider_config,

   577→    models = create_agent_models(

   581→    manager = _create_manager(prompts, models, provider, enable_review_tools, max_content_length)

   583→    return manager

   586→def _extract_rate_limit_detail(error: ModelHTTPError) -> str:
   587→    """Extract a human-readable detail message from a 429 ModelHTTPError body.
   588→
   589→    Args:
   590→        error: The 429 ModelHTTPError to extract detail from.
   591→
   592→    Returns:
   593→        str: Detail message from the error body, or string representation of error.
   594→    """
   595→    body = error.body if isinstance(error.body, dict) else {}
   596→    return body.get("message") or body.get("details") or str(error)  # type: ignore[return-value]

   599→def _handle_model_http_error(error: ModelHTTPError, provider: str, model_name: str) -> NoReturn:
   600→    """Handle non-429 ModelHTTPError with actionable logging. Re-raises the error.
   601→
   602→    For 429 rate-limit errors, callers should handle logging and SystemExit directly.
   603→    This allows sweep runners to catch ModelHTTPError for retry logic.
   604→
   605→    Args:
   606→        error: The ModelHTTPError to handle (expected: non-429).
   607→        provider: Provider name for logging context.
   608→        model_name: Model name for logging context.
   609→
   610→    Raises:
   611→        ModelHTTPError: Always re-raises the original error.
   612→    """
   613→    logger.error(f"HTTP error from model {provider}({model_name}): {error}")
   614→    raise error

   617→async def run_manager(
   618→    manager: Agent[None, BaseModel],
   619→    query: UserPromptType,
   620→    provider: str,
   621→    usage_limits: UsageLimits | None,
   622→    execution_id: str | None = None,
   623→) -> tuple[str, Any]:
   624→    """Asynchronously run the manager with the given query and provider.
   625→
   626→    Auto-instrumented by logfire.instrument_pydantic_ai() - no manual decorators needed.
   627→
   628→    Args:
   629→        manager: The system agent responsible for running the query.
   630→        query: The query to be processed by the manager.
   631→        provider: The provider to be used for the query.
   632→        usage_limits: The usage limits to be applied during the query execution.
   633→        execution_id: Optional pre-generated execution ID. When provided, used
   634→            as-is; otherwise a new ``exec_{hex12}`` ID is generated.
   635→
   636→    Returns:
   637→        Tuple of (execution_id, manager_output) for trace retrieval and evaluation.
   638→    """
   639→    # Initialize trace collection
   640→    trace_collector = get_trace_collector()
   641→    if execution_id is None:
   642→        execution_id = f"exec_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
   643→    trace_collector.start_execution(execution_id)
   644→
   645→    model_obj = getattr(manager, "model", None)
   646→    model_name = (
   647→        model_obj
   648→        if isinstance(model_obj, str)
   649→        else (getattr(model_obj, "model_name", "unknown") if model_obj else "unknown")
   650→    )
   651→    logger.info(f"Researching with {provider}({model_name}) and Topic: {query} ...")
   652→
   653→    try:
   654→        logger.info("Waiting for model response ...")
   655→        # Narrow query type for PydanticAI Agent.run() compatibility
   656→        user_prompt: str | None = query if isinstance(query, str | None) else str(query)
   657→        result = await manager.run(user_prompt=user_prompt, usage_limits=usage_limits)
   658→        logger.info(f"Result: {result}")
   659→        logger.info(f"Usage statistics: {result.usage()}")
   660→
   661→        # Finalize trace collection
   662→        trace_collector.end_execution()
   663→        logger.info(f"Trace collection completed for execution: {execution_id}")
   664→
   665→        return execution_id, result.output
   666→
   667→    except ModelHTTPError as e:
   668→        trace_collector.end_execution()
   669→        if e.status_code == 429:
   670→            detail = _extract_rate_limit_detail(e)
   671→            logger.error(f"Rate limit exceeded for {provider}({model_name}): {detail}")
   672→            raise SystemExit(1) from e
   673→        _handle_model_http_error(e, provider, model_name)
   674→
   675→    except UsageLimitExceeded as e:
   676→        trace_collector.end_execution()
   677→        logger.error(f"Token limit reached for {provider}({model_name}): {e}")
   678→        raise SystemExit(1) from e
   679→
   680→    except Exception as e:
   681→        trace_collector.end_execution()
   682→        logger.error(f"Error in run_manager: {e}")
   683→        raise
   684→
   685→
   686→def _determine_effective_token_limit(
   687→    token_limit: int | None,
   688→    chat_env_config: AppEnv,
   689→    provider_config: ProviderConfig,
   690→) -> int | None:
   691→    """Determine effective token limit with priority: CLI/GUI > env var > config.
   692→
   693→    Args:
   694→        token_limit: Optional CLI/GUI token limit override
   695→        chat_env_config: App environment config with AGENT_TOKEN_LIMIT
   696→        provider_config: Provider config with usage_limits
   697→
   698→    Returns:
   699→        Effective token limit or None if not set
   700→    """
   701→    if token_limit is not None:
   702→        return token_limit
   703→    if chat_env_config.AGENT_TOKEN_LIMIT is not None:
   704→        return chat_env_config.AGENT_TOKEN_LIMIT
   705→    return provider_config.usage_limits
   706→
   707→
   708→def _validate_token_limit(effective_limit: int | None) -> None:
   709→    """Validate token limit bounds (1000-1000000).
   710→
   711→    Args:
   712→        effective_limit: Token limit to validate
   713→
   714→    Raises:
   715→        ValueError: If limit is outside valid range
   716→    """
   717→    if effective_limit is None:
   718→        return
   719→
   720→    if effective_limit < 1000:
   721→        msg = f"Token limit {effective_limit} below minimum 1000"
   722→        logger.error(msg)
   723→        raise ValueError(msg)
   724→
   725→    if effective_limit > 1000000:
   726→        msg = f"Token limit {effective_limit} above maximum 1000000"
   727→        logger.error(msg)
   728→        raise ValueError(msg)
   729→
   730→
   731→def _create_usage_limits(effective_limit: int | None) -> UsageLimits | None:
   732→    """Create UsageLimits object if token limit is set.
   733→
   734→    Args:
   735→        effective_limit: Effective token limit
   736→
   737→    Returns:
   738→        UsageLimits object or None
   739→    """
   740→    if effective_limit is None:
   741→        return None
   742→    return UsageLimits(request_limit=10, total_tokens_limit=effective_limit)
   743→
   744→
   745→def setup_agent_env(
   746→    provider: str,
   747→    query: UserPromptType,
   748→    chat_config: ChatConfig | BaseModel,
   749→    chat_env_config: AppEnv,
   750→    token_limit: int | None = None,
   751→) -> EndpointConfig:
   752→    """
   753→    Sets up the environment for an agent by configuring provider settings, prompts,
   754→    API key, and usage limits.
   755→
   756→    Args:
   757→        provider (str): The name of the provider.
   758→        query (UserPromptType): The messages or queries to be sent to the agent.
   759→        chat_config (ChatConfig | BaseModel): The configuration object containing
   760→            provider and prompt settings.
   761→        chat_env_config (AppEnv): The application environment configuration
   762→            containing API keys.
   763→        token_limit (int | None): Optional token limit override (CLI/GUI param).
   764→            Priority: CLI/GUI > env var > config. Valid range: 1000-1000000.
   765→
   766→    Returns:
   767→        EndpointConfig: The configuration object for the agent.
   768→    """
   769→
   770→    if not isinstance(chat_config, ChatConfig):
   771→        raise TypeError("'chat_config' of invalid type: ChatConfig expected")
   772→
   773→    provider_config = get_provider_config(provider, chat_config.providers)
   774→    prompts = chat_config.prompts
   775→    is_api_key, api_key_msg = get_api_key(provider, chat_env_config)
   776→
   777→    if provider.lower() != "ollama" and not is_api_key:
   778→        msg = f"API key for provider '{provider}' is not set."
   779→        logger.error(msg)
   780→        raise ValueError(msg)
   781→
   782→    # Determine and validate token limit with priority: CLI/GUI > env var > config
   783→    effective_limit = _determine_effective_token_limit(
   784→        token_limit, chat_env_config, provider_config
   785→    )
   786→    _validate_token_limit(effective_limit)
   787→    usage_limits = _create_usage_limits(effective_limit)
   788→
   789→    return EndpointConfig.model_validate(
   790→        {
   791→            "provider": provider,
   792→            "query": query,
   793→            "api_key": api_key_msg,
   794→            "prompts": prompts,
   795→            "provider_config": provider_config,
   796→            "usage_limits": usage_limits,
   797→        }
   798→    )
   799→

    35→def read_paper_pdf(
    36→    ctx: RunContext[None] | None,
    37→    pdf_path: str | Path,
    38→) -> str:
    39→    """Read text content from a PDF file using MarkItDown.
    40→
    41→    Note: MarkItDown extracts the entire PDF content as a single text block.
    42→    Page-level extraction is not supported by the underlying library.
    43→
    44→    Args:
    45→        ctx: RunContext (unused but required for tool compatibility).
    46→        pdf_path: Path to the PDF file.
    47→
    48→    Returns:
    49→        str: Extracted text content from the entire PDF in Markdown format.
    50→
    51→    Raises:
    52→        FileNotFoundError: If the PDF file doesn't exist.
    53→        ValueError: If the file is not a PDF or conversion fails.
    54→    """
    55→    # Reason: LLMs hallucinate URLs for paper PDFs; reject them defensively instead of crashing
    56→    if isinstance(pdf_path, str) and pdf_path.startswith(("http://", "https://")):
    57→        return (
    58→            f"Error: URLs are not supported. "
    59→            f"Use paper_id with get_paper_content instead. Received: {pdf_path}"
    60→        )
    61→
    62→    if isinstance(pdf_path, str):
    63→        pdf_file = Path(pdf_path)
    64→    else:
    65→        pdf_file = pdf_path
    66→    if not pdf_file.exists():
    67→        raise FileNotFoundError(f"PDF file not found: {pdf_file}")
    68→    if pdf_file.suffix.lower() != ".pdf":
    69→        raise ValueError(f"Not a PDF file: {pdf_file}")
    70→
    71→    try:
    72→        md_converter = MarkItDown()
    73→        result = md_converter.convert(pdf_file)
    74→        logger.info(f"Extracted text from {pdf_file}")
    75→        return result.text_content.strip()
    76→
    77→    except Exception as e:
    78→        logger.error(f"Error reading PDF with MarkItDown: {e}")
    79→        raise ValueError(f"Failed to read PDF: {str(e)}")
    80→

    82→T = TypeVar("T")
    83→
    84→
    85→async def _traced_tool_call(  # noqa: UP047
    86→    agent_id: str,
    87→    tool_name: str,
    88→    context: str,
    89→    fn: Callable[[], Awaitable[T]],
    90→    error_cls: type[Exception] = ModelRetry,
    91→    error_msg: str = "",
    92→) -> T:
    93→    """Execute an async tool function with tracing and error handling.
    94→
    95→    Wraps tool business logic with standardized timing, trace logging,
    96→    and error handling to eliminate boilerplate across PeerRead tools.
    97→
    98→    Args:
    99→        agent_id: Agent identifier for trace logging.
   100→        tool_name: Name of the tool being called.
   101→        context: Context string for trace logging (e.g., "paper_id=123").
   102→        fn: Zero-arg async callable containing the tool's business logic.
   103→        error_cls: Exception type to wrap unexpected errors (default: ModelRetry).
   104→        error_msg: Prefix for the error message on unexpected failures.
   105→
   106→    Returns:
   107→        T: The result from the tool's business logic.
   108→
   109→    Raises:
   110→        ModelRetry: Re-raised if the business logic raises it.
   111→        error_cls: Wraps any other exception with the provided error_msg.
   112→    """
   113→    start_time = time.perf_counter()
   114→    trace_collector = get_trace_collector()
   115→    success = False
   116→
   117→    try:
   118→        result = await fn()
   119→        success = True
   120→        return result
   121→    except ModelRetry:
   122→        raise
   123→    except Exception as e:
   124→        logger.error(f"Error in {tool_name}: {e}")
   125→        msg = f"{error_msg}: {str(e)}" if error_msg else str(e)
   126→        raise error_cls(msg)
   127→    finally:
   128→        duration = time.perf_counter() - start_time
   129→        trace_collector.log_tool_call(
   130→            agent_id=agent_id,
   131→            tool_name=tool_name,
   132→            success=success,
   133→            duration=duration,
   134→            context=context,
   135→        )

   213→    @agent.tool

   253→def _truncate_paper_content(abstract: str, body: str, max_length: int) -> str:
   254→    """Truncate paper content to fit within max_length while preserving abstract.
   255→
   256→    Args:
   257→        abstract: The paper abstract (always preserved).
   258→        body: The full body content to be truncated if necessary.
   259→        max_length: Maximum total character length.
   260→
   261→    Returns:
   262→        str: Content with abstract preserved and body truncated if needed.
   263→    """
   264→    # Reason: Always preserve abstract as it contains critical paper summary
   265→    abstract_section = f"Abstract:\n{abstract}\n\n"
   266→    full_content = abstract_section + body
   267→
   268→    if len(full_content) <= max_length:
   269→        return full_content
   270→
   271→    # Calculate available space for body after abstract
   272→    available_for_body = max_length - len(abstract_section) - len("\n[TRUNCATED]")
   273→
   274→    if available_for_body <= 0:
   275→        logger.warning(
   276→            f"Content truncation: abstract alone exceeds max_length. "
   277→            f"Original: {len(full_content)} chars, Limit: {max_length} chars"
   278→        )
   279→        return abstract_section + "[TRUNCATED]"
   280→
   281→    truncated_body = body[:available_for_body]
   282→    result = abstract_section + truncated_body + "\n[TRUNCATED]"
   283→
   284→    logger.warning(
   285→        f"Content truncated: {len(full_content)} chars -> {len(result)} chars (limit: {max_length})"
   286→    )
   287→
   288→    return result
   289→

   291→def _load_paper_content_with_fallback(
   292→    ctx: RunContext[None],
   293→    loader: PeerReadLoader,
   294→    paper_id: str,
   295→    paper_abstract: str,
   296→) -> str:
   297→    """Load paper content with PDF fallback strategy."""
   298→    paper_content = loader.load_parsed_pdf_content(paper_id)
   299→    if paper_content:
   300→        return paper_content
   301→
   302→    logger.warning(f"No parsed PDF content found for paper {paper_id}. Attempting to read raw PDF.")
   303→    raw_pdf_path = loader.get_raw_pdf_path(paper_id)
   304→
   305→    if not raw_pdf_path:
   306→        logger.warning(f"No raw PDF found for paper {paper_id}. Using abstract as fallback.")
   307→        return paper_abstract
   308→
   309→    try:
   310→        paper_content = read_paper_pdf(ctx, raw_pdf_path)
   311→        logger.info(f"Successfully read raw PDF for paper {paper_id}.")
   312→        return paper_content
   313→    except Exception as e:
   314→        logger.warning(
   315→            f"Failed to read raw PDF for paper {paper_id}: {e}. Using abstract as fallback."
   316→        )
   317→        return paper_abstract

   320→def _load_and_format_template(
   321→    paper_title: str,
   322→    paper_abstract: str,
   323→    paper_content: str,
   324→    tone: str,
   325→    review_focus: str,
   326→    max_content_length: int,
   327→) -> str:
   328→    """Load review template and format with paper information.
   329→
   330→    Args:
   331→        paper_title: Title of the paper.
   332→        paper_abstract: Abstract of the paper.
   333→        paper_content: Full body content of the paper.
   334→        tone: Review tone.
   335→        review_focus: Review focus type.
   336→        max_content_length: Maximum content length for truncation.
   337→
   338→    Returns:
   339→        str: Formatted review template with truncated content if needed.
   340→    """
   341→    template_path = get_review_template_path()
   342→
   343→    try:
   344→        with open(template_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
   345→            template_content = f.read()
   346→
   347→        # Truncate paper content before formatting into template
   348→        truncated_content = _truncate_paper_content(
   349→            paper_abstract, paper_content, max_content_length
   350→        )
   351→
   352→        # Sanitize user-controlled content before template formatting
   353→        # This prevents format string injection attacks while preserving template compatibility
   354→        sanitized_title = sanitize_paper_title(paper_title)
   355→        sanitized_abstract = sanitize_paper_abstract(paper_abstract)
   356→        sanitized_content = sanitize_paper_content(truncated_content)
   357→
   358→        # Reason: Safe to use .format() — adversary-controlled inputs are sanitized:
   359→        # - title/abstract: XML-wrapped (length-limited)
   360→        # - paper content: braces escaped + XML-wrapped (prevents format string injection)
   361→        # - tone/review_focus: agent-controlled, not adversary input
   362→        return template_content.format(
   363→            paper_title=sanitized_title,
   364→            paper_abstract=sanitized_abstract,
   365→            paper_full_content=sanitized_content,
   366→            tone=tone,
   367→            review_focus=review_focus,
   368→        )
   369→    except FileNotFoundError:
   370→        logger.error(f"Review template file not found at {template_path}")
   371→        raise ValueError(f"Review template configuration file missing: {template_path}")
   372→    except Exception as e:
   373→        logger.error(f"Error loading review template: {e}")
   374→        raise ValueError(f"Failed to load review template: {str(e)}")

   377→def add_peerread_review_tools_to_agent(
   378→    agent: Agent[None, BaseModel],
   379→    agent_id: str = "manager",
   380→    max_content_length: int = 15000,
   381→):
   382→    """Add PeerRead review generation and persistence tools to an agent.
   383→
   384→    Args:
   385→        agent: The agent to which review tools will be added.
   386→        agent_id: The agent identifier for tracing (default: "manager").
   387→        max_content_length: The maximum number of characters to include in the prompt.
   388→    """
   389→
   390→    @agent.tool
   391→    async def generate_paper_review_content_from_template(  # type: ignore[reportUnusedFunction]
   392→        ctx: RunContext[None],
   393→        paper_id: str,
   394→        review_focus: str = "comprehensive",
   395→        tone: str = "professional",
   396→    ) -> str:
   397→        """Create a review template for a specific paper.
   398→
   399→        Only call this tool when the user explicitly asks to review a paper.
   400→        Do NOT call for greetings, general questions, or non-paper queries.
   401→
   402→        WARNING: This function does NOT generate actual reviews. It creates a
   403→        structured template that would need to be filled in manually or by
   404→        another AI system. This is a demonstration/template function only.
   405→
   406→        Args:
   407→            paper_id: Unique identifier for the paper being reviewed.
   408→            review_focus: Type of review (comprehensive, technical, high-level).
   409→            tone: Tone of the review (professional, constructive, critical).
   410→
   411→        Returns:
   412→            str: Review template with paper information and placeholder sections
   413→                 that need to be manually completed.
   414→        """
   415→
   416→        async def _fn() -> str:
   417→            config = load_peerread_config()
   418→            loader = PeerReadLoader(config)
   419→            paper = loader.get_paper_by_id(paper_id)
   420→            if not paper:
   421→                raise ModelRetry(f"Paper {paper_id} not found in PeerRead dataset")
   422→            paper_content = _load_paper_content_with_fallback(ctx, loader, paper_id, paper.abstract)
   423→            review_template = _load_and_format_template(
   424→                paper.title, paper.abstract, paper_content, tone, review_focus, max_content_length
   425→            )
   426→            logger.info(
   427→                f"Created review template for paper {paper_id} "
   428→                f"(intermediate step, requires agent completion)"
   429→            )
   430→            return review_template
   431→
   432→        return await _traced_tool_call(
   433→            agent_id=agent_id,
   434→            tool_name="generate_paper_review_content_from_template",
   435→            context=f"paper_id={paper_id},focus={review_focus}",
   436→            fn=_fn,
   437→            error_msg="Failed to create review template",
   438→        )
   439→
   440→    @agent.tool
   441→    async def save_paper_review(  # type: ignore[reportUnusedFunction]
   442→        ctx: RunContext[None],
   443→        paper_id: str,
   444→        review_text: str,
   445→        recommendation: str = "",
   446→        confidence: float = 0.0,
   447→    ) -> str:
   448→        """Save agent-generated review to persistent storage.
   449→
   450→        Only call this tool after generating a review the user requested.
   451→        Do NOT call for greetings, general questions, or non-paper queries.
   452→
   453→        Args:
   454→            paper_id: Unique identifier for the paper being reviewed.
   455→            review_text: Review text generated by the agent.
   456→            recommendation: Review recommendation (accept/reject/etc).
   457→            confidence: Confidence score for the review (0.0-1.0).
   458→
   459→        Returns:
   460→            str: Path to the saved review file.
   461→        """
   462→
   463→        async def _fn() -> str:
   464→            from app.utils.run_context import get_active_run_context
   465→
   466→            run_ctx = get_active_run_context()
   467→            review = PeerReadReview(
   468→                comments=review_text,
   469→                recommendation=recommendation if recommendation else "UNKNOWN",
   470→                reviewer_confidence=str(confidence) if confidence > 0 else "UNKNOWN",
   471→            )
   472→            persistence = ReviewPersistence()
   473→            filepath = persistence.save_review(
   474→                paper_id, review, run_dir=run_ctx.run_dir if run_ctx else None
   475→            )
   476→            logger.info(f"Saved review for paper {paper_id} to {filepath}")
   477→            return filepath
   478→
   479→        return await _traced_tool_call(
   480→            agent_id=agent_id,
   481→            tool_name="save_paper_review",
   482→            context=f"paper_id={paper_id}",
   483→            fn=_fn,
   484→            error_cls=ValueError,
   485→            error_msg="Failed to save review",
   486→        )
   487→
   488→    @agent.tool
   489→    async def save_structured_review(  # type: ignore[reportUnusedFunction]
   490→        ctx: RunContext[None],
   491→        paper_id: str,
   492→        structured_review: GeneratedReview,
   493→    ) -> str:
   494→        """Save a structured review object to persistent storage.
   495→
   496→        Only call this tool after generating a review the user requested.
   497→        Do NOT call for greetings, general questions, or non-paper queries.
   498→
   499→        Args:
   500→            paper_id: Unique identifier for the paper being reviewed.
   501→            structured_review: GeneratedReview object with validated fields.
   502→
   503→        Returns:
   504→            str: Path to the saved review file.
   505→        """
   506→
   507→        # Reason: derive model_info from actual model name instead of hardcoding
   508→        agent_model = agent.model
   509→        resolved_name = (
   510→            agent_model
   511→            if isinstance(agent_model, str)
   512→            else (getattr(agent_model, "model_name", "unknown") if agent_model else "unknown")
   513→        )
   514→        model_info = f"{resolved_name} via PydanticAI"
   515→
   516→        async def _fn() -> str:
   517→            from datetime import UTC, datetime
   518→
   519→            from app.utils.run_context import get_active_run_context
   520→
   521→            run_ctx = get_active_run_context()
   522→            peerread_format = structured_review.to_peerread_format()
   523→            review = PeerReadReview.model_validate(peerread_format)
   524→
   525→            persistence = ReviewPersistence()
   526→            filepath = persistence.save_review(
   527→                paper_id,
   528→                review,
   529→                run_dir=run_ctx.run_dir if run_ctx else None,
   530→                structured_review=structured_review.model_dump(),
   531→                model_info=model_info,
   532→            )
   533→
   534→            timestamp = datetime.now(UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ")
   535→            ReviewGenerationResult(
   536→                paper_id=paper_id,
   537→                review=structured_review,
   538→                timestamp=timestamp,
   539→                model_info=model_info,
   540→            )
   541→
   542→            logger.info(f"Saved review for paper {paper_id} to {filepath}")
   543→            return filepath
   544→
   545→        return await _traced_tool_call(
   546→            agent_id=agent_id,
   547→            tool_name="save_structured_review",
   548→            context=f"paper_id={paper_id}",
   549→            fn=_fn,
   550→            error_cls=ValueError,
   551→            error_msg="Failed to save structured review",
   552→        )

1→"""
     2→PeerRead dataset data models.
     3→
     4→This module defines Pydantic models for representing PeerRead scientific paper
     5→review data structures. These models ensure type safety and validation for
     6→papers, reviews, and evaluation results used in the multi-agent system evaluation.
     7→
     8→The models are based on the actual PeerRead dataset structure validated from:
     9→https://raw.githubusercontent.com/allenai/PeerRead/master/data/acl_2017/train/reviews/104.json
    10→
    11→This module also includes structured data models for LLM-generated reviews,
    12→ensuring consistency and validation against the PeerRead format.
    13→"""
    14→
    15→import re
    16→from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal
    17→
    18→from pydantic import BaseModel, BeforeValidator, ConfigDict, Field, field_validator
    19→
    20→# Coerce numeric score values from raw PeerRead JSON (int) to str.
    21→# Reason: Some PeerRead JSON files store scores as integers (e.g., "SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS": 3)
    22→# which fail str validation without coercion.
    23→_ScoreStr = Annotated[str, BeforeValidator(str)]
    24→
    25→# Recommendation word → numeric score mapping for weak-structured-output providers (e.g. Cerebras).
    26→_WORD_TO_SCORE: dict[str, int] = {
    27→    "strong accept": 5,
    28→    "strong_accept": 5,
    29→    "accept": 4,
    30→    "borderline accept": 3,
    31→    "borderline reject": 3,
    32→    "borderline": 3,
    33→    "reject": 2,
    34→    "strong reject": 1,
    35→    "strong_reject": 1,
    36→}
    37→
    38→
    39→def _coerce_score_to_int(v: Any) -> Any:
    40→    """Coerce LLM score values to int for providers that ignore integer schema constraints.
    41→
    42→    Reason: Providers like Cerebras with openai_supports_strict_tool_definition=False
    43→    may return natural language descriptions, floats, or word labels instead of integers.
    44→    Extraction priority: word mapping → float rounding → first digit in text → default 3.
    45→    """
    46→    if isinstance(v, int):
    47→        return v
    48→    if isinstance(v, float):
    49→        return max(1, min(5, round(v)))
    50→    if isinstance(v, str):
    51→        v_lower = v.lower().strip()
    52→        if v_lower in _WORD_TO_SCORE:
    53→            return _WORD_TO_SCORE[v_lower]
    54→        try:
    55→            return max(1, min(5, round(float(v_lower.split()[0]))))
    56→        except (ValueError, IndexError):
    57→            pass
    58→        if m := re.search(r"\b([1-5])\b", v):
    59→            return int(m.group(1))
    60→        return 3
    61→    return v
    62→
    63→
    64→def _coerce_presentation_format(v: Any) -> Any:
    65→    """Coerce presentation format to Literal['Poster', 'Oral'].
    66→
    67→    Reason: Same provider compliance issue — model may return a sentence describing
    68→    the format instead of the exact literal value.
    69→    """
    70→    if isinstance(v, str) and v not in ("Poster", "Oral"):
    71→        return "Oral" if "oral" in v.lower() else "Poster"
    72→    return v
    73→
    74→
    75→_ScoreInt = Annotated[int, BeforeValidator(_coerce_score_to_int)]
    76→_PresentationFormatLiteral = Annotated[
    77→    Literal["Poster", "Oral"], BeforeValidator(_coerce_presentation_format)
    78→]
    79→
    80→
    81→class PeerReadReview(BaseModel):
    82→    """Individual peer review from PeerRead dataset.
    83→
    84→    Note: Some PeerRead papers (e.g., 304-308, 330) lack optional fields.
    85→    Defaults to "UNKNOWN" for missing review criteria fields.
    86→
    87→    Accepts both PeerRead uppercase keys (IMPACT) and model lowercase keys
    88→    (impact) via populate_by_name with aliases. Numeric score fields are
    89→    coerced to str to handle raw PeerRead JSON integer values.
    90→    """
    91→
    92→    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
    93→
    94→    impact: _ScoreStr = Field(
    95→        default="UNKNOWN", validation_alias="IMPACT", description="Impact score (1-5)"
    96→    )
    97→    substance: _ScoreStr = Field(
    98→        default="UNKNOWN", validation_alias="SUBSTANCE", description="Substance score (1-5)"
    99→    )
   100→    appropriateness: _ScoreStr = Field(
   101→        default="UNKNOWN",
   102→        validation_alias="APPROPRIATENESS",
   103→        description="Appropriateness score (1-5)",
   104→    )
   105→    meaningful_comparison: _ScoreStr = Field(
   106→        default="UNKNOWN",
   107→        validation_alias="MEANINGFUL_COMPARISON",
   108→        description="Meaningful comparison score (1-5)",
   109→    )
   110→    presentation_format: str = Field(
   111→        default="Poster",
   112→        validation_alias="PRESENTATION_FORMAT",
   113→        description="Presentation format (Poster/Oral)",
   114→    )
   115→    comments: str = Field(default="", description="Detailed review comments")
   116→    soundness_correctness: _ScoreStr = Field(
   117→        default="UNKNOWN",
   118→        validation_alias="SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS",
   119→        description="Soundness/correctness score (1-5)",
   120→    )
   121→    originality: _ScoreStr = Field(
   122→        default="UNKNOWN", validation_alias="ORIGINALITY", description="Originality score (1-5)"
   123→    )
   124→    recommendation: _ScoreStr = Field(
   125→        default="UNKNOWN",
   126→        validation_alias="RECOMMENDATION",
   127→        description="Overall recommendation score (1-5)",
   128→    )
   129→    clarity: _ScoreStr = Field(
   130→        default="UNKNOWN", validation_alias="CLARITY", description="Clarity score (1-5)"
   131→    )
   132→    reviewer_confidence: _ScoreStr = Field(
   133→        default="UNKNOWN",
   134→        validation_alias="REVIEWER_CONFIDENCE",
   135→        description="Reviewer confidence score (1-5)",
   136→    )
   137→    is_meta_review: bool | None = Field(default=None, description="Whether this is a meta review")
   138→
   139→    def is_compliant(self) -> bool:
   140→        """Check if all score fields are populated (not UNKNOWN).
   141→
   142→        A review is compliant when every field that defaults to UNKNOWN
   143→        has been populated with an actual value from the raw JSON.
   144→
   145→        Returns:
   146→            True if all score fields have non-UNKNOWN values.
   147→        """
   148→        # Reason: Derive dynamically from model_fields to stay in sync with field definitions.
   149→        return all(
   150→            getattr(self, name) != "UNKNOWN"
   151→            for name, info in PeerReadReview.model_fields.items()
   152→            if info.default == "UNKNOWN"
   153→        )
   154→

   177→class GeneratedReview(BaseModel):
   178→    """
   179→    Structured data model for LLM-generated reviews.
   180→
   181→    This model enforces the PeerRead review format and ensures
   182→    all required fields are present with proper validation.
   183→    """
   184→
   185→    impact: _ScoreInt = Field(
   186→        ..., ge=1, le=5, description="Impact rating (1=minimal, 5=high impact)"
   187→    )
   188→
   189→    substance: _ScoreInt = Field(
   190→        ..., ge=1, le=5, description="Substance/depth rating (1=shallow, 5=substantial)"
   191→    )
   192→
   193→    appropriateness: _ScoreInt = Field(
   194→        ...,
   195→        ge=1,
   196→        le=5,
   197→        description="Venue appropriateness rating (1=inappropriate, 5=appropriate)",
   198→    )
   199→
   200→    meaningful_comparison: _ScoreInt = Field(
   201→        ...,
   202→        ge=1,
   203→        le=5,
   204→        description="Related work comparison rating (1=poor, 5=excellent)",
   205→    )
   206→
   207→    presentation_format: _PresentationFormatLiteral = Field(
   208→        ..., description="Recommended presentation format"
   209→    )
   210→
   211→    comments: str = Field(
   212→        ...,
   213→        min_length=100,
   214→        description="Detailed review comments covering contributions, strengths, "
   215→        "weaknesses, technical soundness, clarity, and suggestions",
   216→    )
   217→
   218→    soundness_correctness: _ScoreInt = Field(
   219→        ...,
   220→        ge=1,
   221→        le=5,
   222→        description="Technical soundness rating (1=many errors, 5=very sound)",
   223→    )
   224→
   225→    originality: _ScoreInt = Field(
   226→        ...,
   227→        ge=1,
   228→        le=5,
   229→        description="Originality rating (1=not original, 5=highly original)",
   230→    )
   231→
   232→    recommendation: _ScoreInt = Field(
   233→        ...,
   234→        ge=1,
   235→        le=5,
   236→        description=(
   237→            "Overall recommendation (1=strong reject, 2=reject, 3=borderline, "
   238→            "4=accept, 5=strong accept)"
   239→        ),
   240→    )
   241→
   242→    clarity: _ScoreInt = Field(
   243→        ...,
   244→        ge=1,
   245→        le=5,
   246→        description="Presentation clarity rating (1=very unclear, 5=very clear)",
   247→    )
   248→
   249→    reviewer_confidence: _ScoreInt = Field(
   250→        ...,
   251→        ge=1,
   252→        le=5,
   253→        description="Reviewer confidence rating (1=low confidence, 5=high confidence)",
   254→    )
   255→
   256→    @field_validator("comments")
   257→    def validate_comments_structure(cls, v: str) -> str:  # noqa: N805
   258→        """Ensure comments contain key review sections."""
   259→        required_sections = [
   260→            "contributions",
   261→            "strengths",
   262→            "weaknesses",
   263→            "technical",
   264→            "clarity",
   265→        ]
   266→
   267→        v_lower = v.lower()
   268→        missing_sections = [section for section in required_sections if section not in v_lower]
   269→
   270→        if missing_sections:
   271→            # Just warn but don't fail - LLM might use different wording
   272→            pass
   273→
   274→        return v
   275→
   276→    def to_peerread_format(self) -> dict[str, str | None]:
   277→        """Convert to PeerRead dataset format for compatibility."""
   278→        return {
   279→            "IMPACT": str(self.impact),
   280→            "SUBSTANCE": str(self.substance),
   281→            "APPROPRIATENESS": str(self.appropriateness),
   282→            "MEANINGFUL_COMPARISON": str(self.meaningful_comparison),
   283→            "PRESENTATION_FORMAT": self.presentation_format,
   284→            "comments": self.comments,
   285→            "SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS": str(self.soundness_correctness),
   286→            "ORIGINALITY": str(self.originality),
   287→            "RECOMMENDATION": str(self.recommendation),
   288→            "CLARITY": str(self.clarity),
   289→            "REVIEWER_CONFIDENCE": str(self.reviewer_confidence),
   290→            "is_meta_review": None,
   291→        }
